questions: Can we travel to parallel universes?
answers: A parallel universe is a hypothetical self-contained reality coexisting with our own. The multiverse theory suggests infinite universes may exist with different physical laws, histories, and versions of ourselves.

The many-worlds interpretation of quantum mechanics suggests that every quantum decision creates a new universe branch. While mathematically elegant, there's currently no way to prove or disprove the existence of parallel universes.
Parallel universe holo amader universe er pashapashi arekta universe jar nijosso reality ache. Multiverse theory onujayi, infinite universes thakte pare jekhane different version er amra achi.

The multiverse concept appears in quantum physics, string theory, and cosmology. While fascinating and mathematically supported by some theories, we currently have no experimental evidence of other universes. It remains one of physics' most intriguing open questions.
